what_it_does: Implements a read-only query for staged principle candidates and a deliberately unwired ratification seam that throws if invoked, ensuring no model-based ratification or auto-commit happens without explicit human approval.
observations: Uses better-sqlite3 Database type for dependency injection.
listStagedCandidates maps snake_case SQL columns to camelCase TypeScript fields.
ratifyCandidate accepts a candidate ID but ignores the database and always throws RatifyNotWiredError.
Extensive comments frame the file as a gated seam for a future D-2 powerful-judge step.
No actual model call, no insertUnit implementation, and no write to candidates.status is present.
standouts: ratifyCandidate is typed as never and fails loud rather than silently no-oping.
The file is self-documenting about what it refuses to do and what wiring is required when greenlit.
Principles are treated as propositions until explicitly ratified by a human.
conventions: Public TypeScript API uses camelCase while the database schema uses snake_case.
Exported error class follows the naming pattern {Feature}Error.
Heavy JSDoc comments explain architectural intent inline.
Default parameter values are used for query filters.
concerns: ratifyCandidate takes a Database argument it does not use, which may confuse callers expecting a real transaction.
The error message is very long and mixes operational instruction with exception text.
There is no input validation on candidateId before throwing.
No transaction or rollback logic is shown for the future commit path.
notes: A self-described placeholder gate: it lets you list staged candidates but will throw if anything tries to ratify them automatically.
questions: Where is the candidates table schema defined and migrated?
Where does insertUnit live and what is its exact signature?
How will the D-2 judge endpoint be configured and authenticated?
What user interface or workflow surfaces the verdict for Tim's explicit approval?
What does the basis field represent for a staged candidate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a gated, unwired ratification seam, and that is exactly what the code does.
apparent_themes: explicit separation between candidate and committed unit
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a hard boundary so that a powerful model can propose ratifications but cannot commit them without a human explicitly approving, preventing the system from falsely claiming a principle was ratified.
novelty: It is a seam that not only documents its future wiring but actively refuses to operate, making the absence of the judge model a runtime-enforced invariant rather than a missing feature.
contribution: Provides a read-only staging query and a fail-loud ratification seam that makes the human-approval boundary explicit, preserving the invariant that candidates remain propositions until explicitly ratified.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript file implementing a gated principle-ratification gate that lists staged candidates and throws a RatifyNotWiredError if automatic ratification is attempted.
intention_for_embedding: Serve as the controlled integration point for a powerful-judge ratification step that requires explicit human approval before committing candidates to units.

symbols.does: Describes the shape of a candidate row as returned from the candidates table.
symbols.description: A TypeScript interface that maps the SQL columns of a staged candidate to camelCase properties, including id, candidateType, content, sourceAddress, sessionId, roundId, project, status, and basis.

symbols.does: Queries the candidates table for rows matching a given status and returns them as StagedCandidate objects.
symbols.description: A read-only function that prepares a SELECT statement filtering by status (default 'proposed'), orders by created_at, and maps each snake_case result row into the camelCase StagedCandidate interface.

symbols.does: Custom error thrown when the gated ratify seam is invoked before it is wired.
symbols.description: An Error subclass used to fail loudly when ratifyCandidate is called, signaling that the powerful-judge integration is not yet implemented and preventing silent no-op behavior.

symbols.does: Always throws RatifyNotWiredError because the ratification seam is gated and not wired this beat.
symbols.description: A flagged seam function that documents the future D-2 judge ratification flow and refuses to perform any model call, auto-approval, or unit commit, enforcing the no-auto-write invariant.
